item.title: Directors' Deals: Great Portland Estates CFO buys in as share value drops by quarter
item.description: Developer hit by concerns about AI impact on office jobs and rising debt costs in wake of Middle East conflict

item.title: Stockpickers: Raspberry Pi, Princes Group, AG Barr
item.description: Our experts discuss which companies to buy, sell or hold this week

item.title: Go to court and lose out on £9bn car finance redress scheme, says FCA boss
item.description: Comments by Nikhil Rathi position watchdog for stand-off with claims management companies and law firms

item.title: Trump’s tales are still muddling markets
item.description: Continuation of the Middle East war is sapping hopes of a return to more normal conditions after the conflict

item.title: For Easter-egg makers, it is chocolate tomorrow
item.description: Sweeter times lie ahead as the market for cocoa buyers improves but prices take time to feed through the chain

item.title: Fintechs race to lure customers away from UK’s high-street banks
item.description: Wise announced plans to roll out current accounts following Klarna’s pivot to become a full-service bank last year

item.title: Why investment trusts are going big on private equity
item.description: Trusts give retail investors the opportunity to get a slice of highly sought-after private companies — but the trend poses difficult questions about valuation and performance

item.title: Middle East war day 35 as it happened: US launches rescue operation for plane’s missing crew member
item.description: Second aircraft lost near Strait of Hormuz after one of two aboard F-15 is found

item.title: US fighter jet shot down over Iran
item.description: One of the two crew members on the American F-15E aircraft has been rescued while a second warplane has been lost

item.title: Montreal Port Authority CEO abruptly leaves role
item.description: No reason was given for the departure of Julie Gascon

item.title: OpenAI chief operating officer takes on new role in shake-up
item.description: Brad Lightcap assigned fresh responsibilities focusing on special projects as group prepares for an initial public offering

item.title: Meta-backed data centre seeks $3bn for campus with novel financing
item.description: ‘Project Walleye’ lenders would be first to fund both construction and power

item.title: U.S. judge upholds decision to block Fed Chair Jerome Powell subpoenas
item.description: Justice James Boasberg said Washington had presented ‘no evidence whatsoever of fraud’

item.title: Food-company megadeals make rare comeback in first quarter
item.description: Sysco’s and McCormick’s multibillion-dollar merger deals reflect broad reshaping across food industry

item.title: Japanese, French and Omani vessels crossed Strait of Hormuz since Thursday, data shows
item.description: Crossings reflect Iran’s policy to allow passage for vessels with no U.S. or Israeli links

item.title: The great Wall Street relocation that never quite happens
item.description: While Apollo seeks a second headquarters in the American south, demand for office space rises in New York

item.title: In the Iran disaster, Trump’s only choice is a recession or a worse recession
item.description: Whether the President likes it or not, the U.S. is integrated with the global oil market

item.title: Pitching in: Non-profit JMG Legacy sprang from a simple love of golf
item.description: James Goodridge and his brother Chris organize a golf pool that has grown to more than 4,000 participants, with proceeds going to various charities

item.title: How analysts and investors are weighing the impact of AI - beyond the scare trade
item.description: Even companies that benefit could be exposed to risks from technologies outside their control

item.title: Canada, China sign pledge in Beijing to deepen financial-sector ties
item.description: Canada’s Finance Minister says he raised the importance of labour standards during meetings with top Chinese officials

item.title: Trump’s budget proposes increasing defence spending by $500-billion while cutting elsewhere
item.description: A 10% cut to discretionary spending requires U.S. Congressional approval and reflects Washington’s priorities going into the 2026 midterms

item.title: Starmer’s cost of living tsar calls for rethink on fuel duty rise
item.description: Comments by Richard Walker come as UK motorists face £2-a-litre diesel as Iran war drives wholesale prices to 4-year high

item.title: U.S. economy adds 178,000 jobs in March, unemployment rate dips to 4.3%
item.description: Hiring rebounded from a loss of 133,000 jobs in February, with health care leading job growth

item.title: Forget Trump, I’m now fully invested again
item.description: Why right now seems like the right time for me to move out of cash

item.title: Young Canadians are increasingly miserable. Government priorities show why
item.description: Governments have not matched the rise in spending on boomers with new revenue from that generation

item.title: Investors are betting on AI chaos. History suggests otherwise
item.description: The experience of past tech revolutions suggests savvy incumbents might muddle through and even thrive

item.title: Management consultants are ruining UK universities
item.description: Relentless off-the-peg commercial rewiring has undermined British higher education

item.title: Rachel Reeves’ ‘mansion tax’ to hit about 160,000 homes, says fiscal watchdog
item.description: OBR analysis estimates one-fifth of affected property owners will appeal revaluations Tax

item.title: Turbulent markets fuel record year for Alex Gerko’s XTX
item.description: Billionaire’s trading business is one of the most profitable UK private companies

item.title: French-owned container ship and 3 Omani tankers pass through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: CMA CGM vessel is first owned by major western line to make safe passage since Iran war began

item.title: BHP’s new potash mine is a test case for Canada in how to build big
item.description: Mining giant is sinking more than $18-billion into its Jansen mine in Saskatchewan as it shifts to future-facing commodities

item.description: In expediting access to the region’s critical minerals, the province may also be accelerating environmental and financial risks

item.description: Test your recollection of the top stories of the week ending April 3

item.description: If policymakers really want an active retail investing culture, they should remove the frictions

item.title: The hedge fund billionaire battle royale
item.description: The Two Sigma co-chief executive appointed to quell the feud between its founders quietly quit last month

item.title: Europe must prepare for ‘long-lasting’ energy shock, EU warns
item.description: Energy commissioner says bloc is assessing fuel rationing and releasing more oil from strategic reserves

item.description: Intervention comes as political pressure rises for British government response to energy crisis caused by Iran war

item.description: AI disruption and war in the Middle East send market towards Treasuries and investment-grade debt

item.description: Manufacturers try to recoup some losses after margins were squeezed by spike in cocoa prices

item.description: Industry explores raising capital from alternative investors to cover AI mega-projects

item.description: Skies turned blood red in Western Australia ahead of tropical system that travelled for more than 6,500km

item.description: Authorities seek intense competition from the US, China and Australia for ‘poster child’ project

item.title: Fund managers snap up bonds on growth threat from Iran war
item.description: After sharp market sell-off, debt investors shift focus from inflation fears to likely damage to economies

item.title: The gas price shock isn’t hitting Canadian cities evenly
item.description: Cities that had higher prices from the start – particularly Vancouver and Victoria – experienced smaller price shocks

item.title: Ottawa, drug companies launch task force to prepare for USMCA talks
item.description: Group plans to meet every two weeks and prepare a report in 90 days with options for Canada’s response

item.title: Nicola Wealth halts investor redemptions on real estate value fund, plans to wind it down
item.description: Fund is latest to curtail redemptions as investors sour on private markets

item.title: Oil prices are soaring, and so are fixed mortgage rates. Should you go variable?
item.description: Anyone taking out a floating rate must have the risk tolerance and budget padding to absorb potentially higher payments

item.title: Industry Minister rejects Stellantis plan to build Chinese EVs at idled Brampton plant
item.description: Mélanie Joly says production at plant must be supported by Ontario and Unifor union

item.title: Canadian regulators considering tighter rules on prediction markets
item.description: Regulators have largely restricted Canadians’ access to the markets, which let users bet on real-world events

item.title: Government-run grocery stores: A leftist idea coming from the heart, not the head
item.description: Even with all the taxpayer money needed to run such a scheme, it’s a fantasy to believe it could meaningfully address affordability issues

item.description: Country posted a services trade surplus of $400-million as exports rose 1.5%, Statscan says

item.description: Weakness in industries hit hard by tariffs could be spilling over into services and other sectors, economists say
